metadata.title: 📚 KSLearn — Complete Learning Platform
metadata.description: All-in-one kslearn content: notes, quizzes, flashcards, and tutorials in a single package
metadata.author: kslearn Team

notes.title: Introduction to kslearn
notes.content:
kslearn is a comprehensive educational learning system that runs in your terminal.

🎯 What You Can Do:
• 📚 Study detailed notes on any subject
• 📝 Take interactive quizzes with instant feedback
• 🃏 Review flashcards for quick memorization
• 🎓 Follow step-by-step tutorials
• 🧠 Ask the offline AI tutor questions
• 📊 Track your learning progress

🚀 How It Works:
• All content is loaded from package files
• Study notes first, then test yourself with quizzes
• Use flashcards for quick review sessions
• Tutorials guide you through complex topics step by step

📱 Where It Runs:
• Termux (Android)
• Linux
• macOS
• Windows

⌨️ Getting Started:
• Run 'kslearn' to launch the interactive menu
• Or use CLI commands: kslearn study, kslearn quiz, kslearn flashcards
• Type 'kslearn help' to see all available commands
key_points: kslearn is a terminal-based learning platform
Study notes before taking quizzes for best results
Flashcards are for quick review, tutorials are for deep learning
Works on Android (Termux), Linux, macOS, and Windows

examples.title: Launching kslearn
examples.explanation: Simply type the command and press Enter.
examples.code:
kslearn
# or the short form:
ksl

notes.title: Study Modes Explained
notes.content:
kslearn offers four ways to learn, each designed for a different learning style.

📚 Notes Mode — Read & Understand:
• Detailed explanations with real-world examples
• Key points highlighted for quick review
• Code examples and step-by-step instructions
• Navigate between topics with N (next) and P (previous)

📝 Quiz Mode — Test Your Knowledge:
• Multiple choice questions with instant feedback
• Explanations for both correct and wrong answers
• Score tracking and streak counting
• Retake quizzes to improve your score

🃏 Flashcard Mode — Quick Review:
• Front: question or term
• Back: answer or definition
• Mark as 'Know it' or 'Still learning'
• Fast-paced, great for memorization

🎓 Tutorial Mode — Step-by-Step Lessons:
• Guided lessons that build on each other
• Interactive checkpoints to ensure understanding
• Perfect for beginners learning a new subject

questions.question: What command launches kslearn's interactive menu?
questions.explanation: Simply typing 'kslearn' or the shortcut 'ksl' opens the full interactive menu.

questions.question: What is the recommended study order for best results?
questions.explanation: Study notes to understand the material first, then take quizzes to test and reinforce your knowledge.

questions.question: Which feature stores your learning progress?
questions.explanation: 'My Progress' tracks quiz scores, accuracy, study streaks, and completed topics.

questions.question: What format does kslearn use for its content packages?
questions.explanation: .ksl (KSLearn Package) files are encrypted, signed packages containing notes, quizzes, flashcards, and tutorials.

questions.question: What is the purpose of flashcard mode?
questions.explanation: Flashcards are designed for quick review sessions to help memorize terms, definitions, and key facts.

questions.question: What is spaced repetition?
options: Studying the same topic every hour
Reviewing material at increasing intervals over time
questions.explanation: Spaced repetition means reviewing material after 1 day, then 3 days, then 7 days, etc. This is proven to improve long-term memory retention.

questions.question: What should you do when you get a quiz question wrong?
options: Skip the explanation and move on
Read the explanation, then review the related notes
Guess on the next question faster
questions.explanation: Wrong answers are learning opportunities. Read why the answer is correct, then review the notes to solidify understanding.

questions.question: What's the benefit of study streaks?
options: They build consistent study habits and motivation
They earn you points to spend
questions.explanation: Streaks track consecutive days of study, encouraging you to build a daily learning habit. Consistency beats cramming.

questions.question: Which is the most effective way to study with kslearn?
options: Only take quizzes without reading notes
Read notes, take quizzes, review flashcards, repeat
Read notes once and never review
questions.explanation: The full cycle — read notes → take quizzes → review flashcards → repeat — leverages multiple learning methods for maximum retention.

questions.question: What does the 'Knowledge Brain' feature do?
options: Stores Q&A pairs you can search offline
questions.explanation: The Knowledge Brain stores Q&A pairs, conversations, and learned information that you can search even without internet.

flashcards.back: KSLearn — a terminal-based educational learning platform for studying any subject

flashcards.front: What are the 4 study modes in kslearn?

flashcards.back: An encrypted, signed content package that holds notes, quizzes, flashcards, or tutorials. Only the creator can build .ksl files; users can only load them.
Reviewing material at increasing intervals (1 day, 3 days, 7 days, etc.) to improve long-term memory retention.

flashcards.front: What should you do after getting a quiz question wrong?
flashcards.back: Read the explanation carefully, then review the related notes to understand the concept you missed.
ksl (instead of typing the full 'kslearn' command)

flashcards.front: Where is your study progress saved?
flashcards.back: In the config files — quiz scores, accuracy, streaks, and completed topics are all tracked automatically.

flashcards.front: What's the difference between Notes and Tutorials?
flashcards.back: Notes = comprehensive reference material. Tutorials = guided step-by-step lessons that build on each other progressively.

flashcards.front: Can you study with kslearn offline?
flashcards.back: Yes! All content is stored locally. The Knowledge Brain works offline. Only the AI chat (tgpt) needs internet.
Termux (Android), Linux, macOS, and Windows — it runs anywhere Python 3.7+ is available.

steps.title: Launch kslearn
steps.content: Open your terminal and type the command to start the interactive menu.
steps.tip: The first time you run kslearn, it will create your config files automatically.

steps.title: Browse Study Notes
steps.content: From the main menu, select option 1 (Study Notes) to browse available topics. You'll see a list of all subjects with descriptions.
steps.tip: Start with the first topic and work your way through sequentially for the best learning experience.

steps.title: Read a Topic
steps.content:
Select a topic number to read the full notes. You'll see detailed content, key points, and examples.

Navigation while reading:
  [N] Next topic
  [P] Previous topic
  [Q] Take quiz on this topic
  [A] Ask AI
  [0] Back to topics
steps.tip: Don't rush — read the examples and try to understand each key point before moving on.

steps.title: Test Yourself with a Quiz
steps.content: After studying notes, press Q to take the quiz for that topic. You'll get multiple-choice questions with instant feedback and explanations.
steps.tip: Aim for 80%+ accuracy. If you score lower, review the notes again and retake the quiz.

steps.title: Review with Flashcards
steps.content: Use flashcard mode (F from the main menu) for quick review sessions. Flashcards show a question on the front — tap to reveal the answer.
steps.tip: Flashcards are perfect for studying during short breaks — 5 minutes with flashcards can reinforce a lot of knowledge.

steps.title: Select a Quiz Category
steps.content: From the main menu, select option 2 (Take Quiz). You'll see all available quiz categories. Choose one by number.
steps.tip: Study the notes for that category first for the best quiz results.

steps.title: Choose a Topic
steps.content: Each quiz has multiple topics. Select one, or press R for a random topic.
steps.tip: Start with basics if you're new to the subject.

steps.title: Answer Questions
steps.content:
For each question:
  1. Read the question carefully
  2. Review all four options
  3. Type the number of your answer (1-4)
  4. Get instant feedback with explanation

Type 'q' at any time to end the quiz early.
steps.tip: Don't guess — think through each option before selecting.

steps.title: Review Your Results
steps.content:
After the quiz, you'll see:
  • Number of questions answered
  • Correct answers count
  • Total score
  • Accuracy percentage
  • Best streak

Use this to identify areas you need to study more.
steps.tip: Aim to beat your previous score each time you retake a quiz.
